# example-package

Minimal reference derivation under `pkgs/example-package/` demonstrating canonical Nix package structure for this flake repo. Serves as template baseline alongside [`.default_template.nix`](../../.default_template.nix).

## Contents

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [default.nix](./default.nix) | Single `stdenv.mkDerivation` derivation; builds and installs shell script `example` as executable. |

## Stack

- **Nix** — `stdenv.mkDerivation`, no extra inputs
- **Entry point:** `default.nix`, callable as `{ stdenv }: stdenv.mkDerivation { … }`

## Architecture

Single-attr-set function pattern: accepts `stdenv`, returns derivation. Uses `rec` so `name` self-references `version`.

```
pkgs/example-package/default.nix
  └─ stdenv.mkDerivation
       ├─ name    = "example-package-${version}"
       ├─ version = "1.0"
       ├─ src     = ./.
       ├─ buildPhase   → writes shell script `example`
       └─ installPhase → install -Dm755 example $out
```

Consumed by [`overlay.nix`](../../overlay.nix) and surfaced via [`flake.nix`](../../flake.nix) alongside sibling packages in [`pkgs/`](../).

## Behavioral Contracts

- `buildPhase`: `echo echo Hello World > example` — output file named `example`, content is literal string `echo Hello World`
- `installPhase`: `install -Dm755 example $out` — installs to bare `$out` (not `$out/bin`), mode `755`
- `version`: hardcoded `"1.0"`; `name` derived as `"example-package-1.0"` via `rec`
